mnemonics.title: Switch Syntax Reminder
mnemonics.content: SWITCH: S elect W hich T hing X ecutes T oday - C HOOSE H andler E acle Case = think of 'Case' as 'Choose the path'

acronyms.expansion: If Condition Then Execute ELSE Execute Alternative
DEFAULT: Don't Expect Any Routine Thing - executes when nothing matches

visualTips.visualization: Draw a flowchart with diamond shape for condition, two paths (TRUE goes left/down, FALSE goes right) leading to different rectangles representing code blocks, then merge back
Draw as staircase - each step checks condition, if true take that path, else move to next step down - visualize as descending decision points
Draw as vertical ladder with boxes (cases) - matching value jumps to correct box, then flows down (fall-through) or exits (break). Arrow syntax shows direct jump without flow-down.

analogies.analogy: Like a fork in the road - go left if condition true, go right if false. Only two choices.
Like sequential checkpoints at airport - show passport, if OK go to next checkpoint, if not rejected at first checkpoint. Each checkpoint is a condition.
Like elevator floor buttons - expression is floor number, each case is a floor, default is 'emergency stop' when invalid floor pressed.
Like sliding down stairs without stopping at each step - you keep moving through multiple cases automatically.
Like emergency exit door in elevator - once you exit at your floor (case), you don't continue to other floors.
Like a voting machine - question (condition) and two buttons: YES button returns first value, NO button returns second value.
